Consent Form:
UNIVERSITY OF MICHIGAN
CONSENT TO BE PART OF THIS STUDY

Study Title: Moral Judgments
Study Creator: Gregory Stanley, Ph.D. Candidate, University of Michigan
Faculty Advisor: Jun Zhang, Professor of Psychology, University of Michigan

Thank you for your interest in participating in this experiment. Please read this entire form before deciding whether to take part.

KEY INFORMATION
This study examines how people make judgments about moral responsibility and punishment in short hypothetical scenarios.
 •	What you will do: Read short scenarios, answer questions about blame/wrongness/punishment, complete a brief memory task, and answer brief questionnaires.
 •	Duration: Approximately 30 minutes of continuous participation.
 •	Compensation/Credit: 0.5 SONA credit for completion.
 •	Risks: Some scenarios describe serious injury and may be unpleasant or upsetting. There are no other foreseeable risks beyond everyday life.
 •	Data Security: Responses are collected through Qualtrics and stored securely.
 •	Stopping: You may stop at any time without penalty, but completion is required for SONA credit.
 •	18+ Years Old: You must be 18 years of age or older to participate.

PURPOSE OF THIS STUDY
The purpose of this study is to understand the nuances of how people attribute moral responsibility and punishment.

WHAT WILL HAPPEN TO ME IN THIS STUDY?
 •	Online Participation: This study is completed online via Qualtrics.
 •	Scenarios + Ratings: You will read several short scenarios and rate judgments such as blameworthiness, wrongness, and punishment.
 •	Memory Task: You will be asked to remember a number briefly while answering some questions.
 •	Questionnaires: You will answer short questionnaires about reasoning style and social attitudes, plus basic demographics.
 •	Debriefing: You will receive a debriefing form at the end of the study.

ENDING THE STUDY
At the end of the study, you will be shown debriefing information and instructions for receiving SONA credit. Your responses will be recorded under a random participation code rather than your name. 

IF I WANT TO STOP PARTICIPATING IN THIS STUDY, WHAT SHOULD I DO?
You are free to leave the study at any time, without penalty. If you share your reasons, they may become part of our record. The researchers will keep the information collected about you for the research unless you ask us to delete it from our records. If the researchers have already used your information in a research analysis it will not be possible to remove your information. To leave the study, click out of the browser tab.

HOW WILL THE RESEARCHERS PROTECT MY INFORMATION?

Your name will not be used in conjunction with your responses. SONA provides a random participation code that is used only for crediting purposes. Your de-identified research data may be used in research reports, articles, or presentations, but will not include information that would let others know who you are. De-identified data may be shared for future research. 

CONTACT INFORMATION

Questions, concerns, or the wish to leave a study should be directed to:
Study Creator: Gregory Stanley; Phone: (714) 454-0435; Email: gregstan@umich.edu, 
Faculty Advisor: Jun Zhang; Phone: (734) 763-6161; Email: junz@umich.edu 

To express concern about a study, contact University of Michigan Health Sciences and Behavioral Sciences Institutional Review Board, 2800 Plymouth Road, Building 520, Room 1169 Ann Arbor, MI 48109. Call: 734-936-0933 or toll free 866-936-0933; Fax: 734-936-1852; E-mail: irbhsbs@umich.edu. You can also contact the University of Michigan Compliance Hotline at (866) 990-0111.

The University of Michigan Institutional Review Board Health Sciences and Behavioral Sciences has determined that this platform is exempt from IRB oversight, but individual researchers must submit proof of IRB approval before deploying experiments. 

YOUR CONSENT

I understand that my participation in the studies described above is entirely voluntary, and I hereby agree to be a participant on this platform.
